questions.question: What is the difference between a probability mass function (PMF) and a probability density function (PDF)?
questions.question_zh: 概率质量函数（PMF）和概率密度函数（PDF）有什么区别？
options: PMFs are used for continuous variables, PDFs for discrete variables
PMFs give exact probabilities for discrete outcomes, while PDFs give densities for continuous variables that must be integrated over an interval to get probability
There is no difference; they are the same concept with different names
PMFs always sum to 0.5, PDFs integrate to 1
options_zh: PMF 用于连续变量，PDF 用于离散变量
PMF 给出离散结果的精确概率；PDF 给出连续变量的密度，要在某个区间上积分才能得到概率
二者没有区别，是同一个概念的两种叫法
PMF 总和为 0.5，PDF 积分为 1
questions.explanation: For discrete variables, the PMF gives P(X=k) directly. For continuous variables, the PDF f(x) is a density -- P(a<=X<=b) requires integrating f(x) from a to b. The density at a single point is not a probability.
questions.explanation_zh: 对于离散变量，PMF 直接给出 P(X=k)。对于连续变量，PDF f(x) 是密度，P(a<=X<=b) 需要把 f(x) 从 a 到 b 积分得到。单点的密度本身并不是概率。

questions.question: What does the Central Limit Theorem state?
questions.question_zh: 中心极限定理说的是什么？
options: All data follows a normal distribution
The mean of many independent random samples converges to a normal distribution regardless of the source distribution
Large datasets always have low variance
The probability of rare events decreases as sample size increases
options_zh: 所有数据都服从正态分布
大量独立随机样本的均值会收敛到正态分布，与原分布形状无关
数据集越大方差就越小
样本量增大时罕见事件的概率会降低
questions.explanation: The CLT says that the average of many independent random variables approaches a Gaussian, no matter what the original distribution looks like. This explains why the normal distribution appears everywhere.
questions.explanation_zh: 中心极限定理告诉我们：大量独立随机变量的均值会逼近高斯分布，与原分布是什么样无关。这解释了为什么正态分布随处可见。

questions.question: Why does softmax subtract the maximum logit before exponentiating (the 'softmax trick')?
questions.question_zh: 为什么 softmax 在做 exp 之前要减去最大 logit（即所谓的「softmax trick」）？
options: To make all probabilities equal
To prevent numerical overflow from exponentiating large numbers while producing mathematically identical results
To normalize the logits to have mean zero
To speed up the computation by reducing the number of exponentiations
options_zh: 让所有概率都相等
防止对大数取 exp 时数值溢出，同时数学结果完全一致
把 logits 归一化到均值为 0
通过减少 exp 次数来加速计算
questions.explanation: exp(100) overflows to infinity. Subtracting max(logits) shifts all values so the largest is 0. exp(0)=1 is safe. The subtraction cancels out in the normalization, giving identical probabilities.
questions.explanation_zh: exp(100) 会溢出到正无穷。减去 max(logits) 后，最大值变为 0，exp(0)=1 完全安全。这一步在归一化时会被约掉，得到的概率分布和原来完全一致。

questions.question: Cross-entropy loss for classification simplifies to -log(q(true_class)). What does this mean intuitively?
questions.question_zh: 分类任务的交叉熵 loss 化简后是 -log(q(true_class))。直观上这意味着什么？
options: Multiply the predicted probabilities by the true labels
Penalize the model based on how low its predicted probability is for the correct class -- lower prediction means higher loss
Average the log probabilities across all classes
Compute the entropy of the true distribution
options_zh: 把预测概率与真实标签相乘
根据模型对正确类别给的概率有多低来惩罚它——给得越低，loss 越大
对所有类别的 log 概率取平均
计算真实分布的熵
questions.explanation: If the model predicts 0.9 for the correct class, loss = -log(0.9) = 0.105 (low). If it predicts 0.01, loss = -log(0.01) = 4.6 (high). The loss punishes low confidence in the correct answer.
questions.explanation_zh: 如果模型对正确类别预测 0.9，loss = -log(0.9) = 0.105，很小；如果只预测 0.01，loss = -log(0.01) = 4.6，很大。这个 loss 在惩罚「对正确答案的信心不足」。

questions.question: Why do language models work with log probabilities instead of raw probabilities?
questions.question_zh: 为什么语言模型用 log 概率而不是原始概率？
options: Log probabilities are easier to interpret visually
Multiplying many small probabilities causes numerical underflow to zero; log probabilities convert products to sums, avoiding this
Log probabilities are required by the transformer architecture
Raw probabilities cannot represent values less than 0.01
options_zh: log 概率更容易直观解读
把许多小概率相乘会因数值下溢变成 0；log 概率把乘法变加法，避免这个问题
transformer 架构要求必须用 log 概率
原始概率无法表示小于 0.01 的值
questions.explanation: P(sentence) = P(word1) * P(word2) * ... quickly underflows to 0.0 with float64. Log P(sentence) = log P(word1) + log P(word2) + ... stays in a finite range. Products become sums.
questions.explanation_zh: P(sentence) = P(word1) * P(word2) * ... 即便用 float64 也会很快下溢成 0.0。换成 log P(sentence) = log P(word1) + log P(word2) + ... 后数值范围有限，乘法也变成了加法。
